title: AI for Asia Fellowship 2026: Empowering Asia’s Next Generation of AI Innovators
summary: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming industries, economies, and societies across the globe. From healthcare and education to finance, agriculture, climate innovation, and digital governance, AI is shaping the future of how people live and work. As Asia continues to emerge as a global hub for technological advancement, the need for young, skilled, and visionary AI leaders has never been greater. The AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering aspiring innovators, changemakers, and technology enthusiasts an opportunity to become part of a powerful movement focused on responsible AI development and regional collaboration. Under the inspiring theme: “Navigating AI Together: Empowering Asia’s Next Generation of Innovators” The fellowship aims to bring together talented individuals from across Asia who are passionate about using artificial intelligence to solve real-world challenges and contribute to sustainable development. Applicants are encouraged to apply now and secure their place as an AI for Asia Fellow 2026 . What is the AI for Asia Fellowship 2026? The AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious opportunity designed to empower emerging leaders and innovators in the field of artificial intelligence. The fellowship seeks to create a collaborative ecosystem where young professionals, students, researchers, entrepreneurs, and tech enthusiasts can learn, connect, and contribute to the future of AI in Asia. The program emphasizes: AI innovation Cross-border collaboration Ethical and responsible AI development Leadership development Digital transformation Community-driven solutions The fellowship is expected to serve as a platform for participants to deepen their understanding of AI technologies while building networks with fellow innovators, mentors, experts, and industry leaders from across the continent. Why the AI for Asia Fellowship Matters Asia is home to some of the fastest-growing digital economies in the world. Countries across the continent are investing heavily in emerging technologies, startups, research institutions, and AI-driven solutions. However, with rapid innovation comes critical questions: How can AI be used responsibly? How can innovation remain inclusive? How can young people participate in shaping AI policies and technologies? How can regional collaboration strengthen AI advancement in Asia? The AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 seeks to address these questions by empowering the next generation of AI leaders with knowledge, collaboration opportunities, and leadership skills. Key Benefits of Becoming an AI for Asia Fellow 2026 Selected fellows may gain access to numerous professional and personal development opportunities, including: 1. Access to AI Knowledge and Learning Opportunities Participants may engage in learning experiences focused on: Artificial intelligence fundamentals Emerging AI technologies Ethical AI practices AI policy discussions Innovation ecosystems Technology leadership 2. Networking Opportunities Fellows can connect with: AI experts Researchers Industry leaders Startup founders Policymakers Fellow innovators across Asia Building these networks can open doors to future collaborations, career growth, mentorship, and entrepreneurial opportunities. 3. Leadership Development The fellowship is designed to nurture future leaders capable of: Driving innovation Leading AI initiatives Creating social impact Influencing technology policy Promoting inclusive digital transformation 4. Regional Collaboration Participants will become part of a growing community of innovators working together to solve regional and global challenges through AI-powered solutions. 5. Increased Visibility and Recognition Being selected as an AI for Asia Fellow can strengthen an applicant’s professional profile and demonstrate commitment to innovation and responsible technology development. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for individuals who are passionate about: Artificial intelligence Emerging technologies Innovation Entrepreneurship Research Digital transformation Social impact Community development Applicants who are eager to contribute to the future of AI in Asia and collaborate with like-minded individuals are strongly encouraged to apply. Skills and Interests That May Strengthen Your Application Candidates with the following interests or backgrounds may particularly benefit from the fellowship: Computer science Data science Machine learning Robotics Software development Public policy Technology entrepreneurship Innovation management Digital governance Research and academia However, interdisciplinary applicants with a strong passion for AI and innovation may also find this opportunity highly valuable. How the Fellowship Can Impact Your Career Participating in international and regional fellowship programs often creates long-term career advantages. Fellows may: Gain exposure to emerging industry trends Develop stronger leadership capabilities Build professional credibility Expand regional and global networks Discover new career pathways in AI and technology As AI continues to reshape industries worldwide, opportunities like the AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 can help participants remain competitive and future-ready. Important Reminder Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible and prepare strong applications that clearly demonstrate: Passion for AI and innovation Leadership potential Commitment to collaboration Interest in solving real-world challenges Deadline The official application deadline was not provided in the announcement. Applicants are advised to apply as soon as possible to avoid missing important updates or submission timelines. How to Apply Applicants can apply through the official application platform for the AI for Asia Fellowship 2026. Apply Here: AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 Application For more information and updates, applicants should regularly check the official website; visit here Final Thoughts The AI for Asia Fellowship 2026 represents more than just a fellowship opportunity. It is a platform for visionary young leaders to collaborate, innovate, and shape the future of artificial intelligence across Asia. As the world enters a new era driven by AI technologies, programs like this are essential for ensuring that innovation remains inclusive, ethical, and impactful. If you are passionate about technology, leadership, and creating solutions for the future, this fellowship could be the opportunity that launches your next big journey in AI and innovation. Apply now and become part of Asia’s next generation of AI innovators. For more information about this opportunity, read the official call for applications . Discover more global AI fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post AI for Asia Fellowship 2026: Empowering Asia’s Next Generation of AI Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PhD in Economics at Sapienza University of Rome: A Comprehensive Guide for 2026 Applicants
summary: The Ph.D. in Economics at Sapienza University of Rome —offered through the Department of Economics and Law—is one of Europe’s most rigorous and research‑intensive doctoral programs. Designed for highly motivated students with a strong passion for advanced economic theory, quantitative methods, and empirical research, this four‑year program provides a transformative academic journey that prepares graduates for impactful careers across academia, policy institutions, and the global private sector. Program Overview The Ph.D. in Economics is structured to cultivate independent thinking, analytical depth, and the ability to produce original, high‑quality research. The program emphasizes: Advanced theoretical foundations in microeconomics, macroeconomics, and econometrics Quantitative and empirical methods for analyzing complex economic phenomena Research‑driven training through seminars, workshops, and collaborative projects Close mentorship from faculty members and the Ph.D. Director A dynamic academic environment supported by the Department of Economics and Law Students are encouraged to engage actively with the department’s vibrant research community, participate in academic events, and contribute to ongoing scholarly discussions. Program Structure The four‑year program includes: Coursework Phase Students complete advanced courses in core economic disciplines and quantitative methods. This phase ensures a strong theoretical and methodological foundation. Research Development Phase Students begin shaping their research agenda, identifying potential dissertation topics, and collaborating with faculty mentors. Seminars and Workshops The department hosts regular academic seminars featuring leading economists from around the world. Students are expected to attend and present their work as they progress. Dissertation Phase Under faculty supervision, students conduct original research culminating in a doctoral dissertation that contributes meaningfully to the field of economics. Career Prospects Graduates of the Ph.D. in Economics at Sapienza University are well‑positioned for competitive roles across multiple sectors. Career pathways include: Academic positions in universities and research institutions Research and analytical roles in international organizations Positions in central banks and government agencies Consulting and strategy roles in the private sector Economic analysis roles in financial institutions and think tanks The faculty provides strong support for navigating both academic and non‑academic job markets, ensuring that graduates are equipped to pursue impactful careers globally. Program Leadership and Contact Information Coordinator: Cristiano Cantore Deputy Coordinator: Emanuele Brancati Administrative Staff: Pietro Mercurio Email: pietro.mercurio@uniroma1.it Address: Dipartimento di Economia e Diritto Facoltà di Economia Sapienza – Università di Roma Via del Castro Laurenziano 9 00161 Roma, Italy Important Notice The activities presented on the program’s webpage are fully or partially funded by the Italian National Recovery and Resilience Plan (PNRR) . Details about Sapienza’s PNRR initiatives can be found on the university’s dedicated page. Deadline June 17, 2026 at 14:00 (local time) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more Phd opportunities here The post PhD in Economics at Sapienza University of Rome: A Comprehensive Guide for 2026 Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships 2026/2027 in the UK | Tuition Fees + £15,000 Stipend
summary: The SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships for the 2026/2027 academic year present an outstanding opportunity for students passionate about sustainability, climate action, social justice, public policy, global development, education, and transformative leadership. Offered by SOAS University of London, this prestigious scholarship programme supports students pursuing master’s degrees connected to the 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The initiative is designed to empower future leaders who are committed to creating sustainable solutions for a just, equitable, and regenerative future. For the 2026/27 academic year, four scholarships will be awarded to exceptional students from around the world. Each scholarship covers full tuition fees and provides a maintenance stipend of at least £15,000, enabling recipients to focus on their academic and professional goals without major financial pressure. The scholarship is open to both domestic and international students, making it one of the most accessible sustainability-focused master’s scholarship opportunities currently available in the United Kingdom. About the SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships The SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships support students who are committed to redefining the relationship between humanity and the planet through innovative thinking, responsible action, and bold leadership. Applicants are expected to demonstrate: Academic excellence Strong commitment to sustainability Interest in global development challenges Leadership potential Alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals The scholarship particularly encourages students whose academic interests and future careers contribute to solving global challenges in areas such as: Climate Action Gender Equality Quality Education Public Health Reduced Inequalities Peace and Justice Clean Energy Sustainable Cities Responsible Consumption Environmental Sustainability Students who are passionate about creating long-term social and environmental impact are strongly encouraged to apply. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive: Full tuition fee coverage Minimum maintenance stipend of £15,000 Opportunity to study in the United Kingdom Access to a globally recognized academic institution International exposure and networking opportunities Opportunity to contribute to global sustainability discussions and initiatives Please note that the scholarship does not cover pre-sessional courses. Eligible Programmes The scholarship is available for: Any eligible full-time 1-year taught Master’s programme linked to sustainable development goals at SOAS University of London. Applicants should ensure that their chosen programme clearly aligns with sustainability themes and the objectives of the SDGs. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Be new admissions applicants for programmes beginning in 2026/27 Hold or expect to obtain a first-class honours degree or international equivalent Demonstrate top academic standing through transcripts and references Have applied to SOAS University of London before applying for the scholarship Receive an unconditional offer before the scholarship selection panel meets Applicants with a 2:1 degree classification may still apply but may not receive preference during selection. Selection Process Applications will be assessed by a scholarship panel based on: Academic merit Leadership potential Commitment to sustainability Alignment between the chosen programme and sustainable development goals Personal statement quality Academic references and transcripts The panel will review all submitted documents carefully. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Step 1: Apply for an Eligible Programme Applicants must first apply for an eligible Master’s programme at SOAS University of London. Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship Scholarship applications will open on 14 May 2026 through the admissions portal. Applicants can only access the scholarship application after applying for a course and receiving login credentials for the admissions portal. Application Deadline: 12 June 2026 at 12 noon (UK local time) Applicants are encouraged to apply early because admissions processing may take several weeks, especially during busy periods. Notification of Results The scholarship shortlisting and awarding process is expected to be completed from July 2026 onward. Successful candidates will be contacted first before notifications are sent to unsuccessful applicants. Why You Should Apply The SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships are more than just financial aid opportunities. They are investments in future changemakers who are committed to solving global challenges and advancing sustainable development. Students interested in climate action, public health, development studies, environmental sustainability, human rights, education, social justice, and policy reform should strongly consider applying for this opportunity. This scholarship offers not only financial support but also the opportunity to study in an internationally respected institution with a strong focus on global impact and social transformation. Application Deadline: 12 June 2026 at 12 noon (UK local time) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity , click here. For more global opportunities, click here. The post Fully Funded SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships 2026/2027 in the UK | Tuition Fees + £15,000 Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Leeds Business School Funded PhD Studentships 2026: Apply for Two Full-Time Research Scholarships at Leeds Beckett University
summary: Leeds Business School is inviting applications for two funded PhD studentships for full-time doctoral study starting in October 2026 . This is an excellent opportunity for ambitious, research-driven individuals who want to pursue a PhD in a dynamic business school environment while contributing to applied research in business, management, society, and economic development. The funded PhD studentships are offered by Leeds Business School , part of Leeds Beckett University , and are designed to support high-quality doctoral research that aligns with the School’s key research interests. Unlike many PhD funding opportunities, these studentships are not attached to one fixed research project . Instead, applicants are expected to develop and submit their own research proposal within the priority areas identified by the School. Successful candidates will study full-time in Leeds and become part of a strong research community made up of academics, researchers, supervisors, and doctoral students. The opportunity is ideal for individuals who are ready to develop original research, contribute to knowledge in business and management, and build a strong academic or research-focused career. About Leeds Business School Leeds Business School is an important part of Leeds Beckett University , located at the University’s City Campus in the heart of Leeds . The School is known for its applied approach to business research, working across a wide range of disciplines connected to business, management, economics, journalism, enterprise, governance, sustainability, and society. The School conducts research across six subject areas , covering many aspects of business and management practice. Its research is both academic and practical, with strong links to real-world organisations and communities. Leeds Business School works with a wide range of partners, including: Businesses of different sizes, from micro-enterprises to multinational companies Local authorities NHS Trusts Other universities Commercial and funded research partners Community and public sector organisations Through these partnerships, the School supports research that is not only theoretical but also practical, relevant, and useful to society. The funded PhD studentships are part of the School’s wider goal to strengthen its research activity, expand its doctoral offering, and increase its research profile in core areas. About the Funded PhD Studentships Leeds Business School is offering two funded PhD studentships for students who wish to study full-time from October 2026 . These studentships are designed for applicants who can propose a research project that fits closely with the School’s research priorities. Because the awards are not tied to a pre-designed project, applicants have the opportunity to shape their own research direction while still working within the School’s strategic areas of interest. This makes the opportunity suitable for candidates who already have a strong research idea and want to develop it under the guidance of experienced academic supervisors. Research Areas for the Leeds Business School PhD Studentships Applicants must submit proposals that directly align with one or more of the key research areas listed by Leeds Business School. Proposals that do not address one of these areas will not be considered. The priority research areas are: 1. Banking and Markets This area may include research related to financial institutions, banking systems, investment markets, capital markets, risk, regulation, financial behaviour, and the role of markets in economic development. Applicants interested in this theme may consider research questions connected to: Banking systems and financial stability Market behaviour and investment trends Financial inclusion Risk management Regulation and governance in financial markets The role of banking in economic growth 2. Sustainability Sustainability is a major research area for business schools globally, especially as organisations face increasing pressure to balance profitability with environmental and social responsibility. Possible research topics may include: Sustainable business models Corporate environmental responsibility Climate-conscious management practices Sustainability reporting Green finance Sustainable supply chains Responsible consumption and production 3. Digital Media and Society This area focuses on the relationship between digital communication, media, technology, and society. It is especially relevant in a world where digital platforms shape public opinion, business communication, culture, journalism, and consumer behaviour. Research proposals may explore: Digital journalism Social media and society Online misinformation Digital communication strategies Media platforms and public engagement Technology and social change The role of digital media in business and communities 4. Responsible Governance and Organisational Behaviour This area is suited to applicants interested in leadership, ethics, accountability, workplace culture, organisational performance, and responsible management. Potential research themes may include: Ethical leadership Corporate governance Organisational culture Employee behaviour and motivation Workplace inclusion Responsible management practices Leadership and accountability in organisations 5. Supply Chain and Anchor Networks This research area focuses on supply chains, networks, regional economies, procurement, logistics, and the role of anchor institutions in supporting local development. Applicants may consider topics such as: Supply chain resilience Local procurement Anchor institutions and regional development Logistics and business networks Sustainable supply chains Public sector procurement Supply chain risk and disruption 6. SME Development and Enterprise This area is ideal for applicants interested in entrepreneurship, small business growth, innovation, enterprise support, and local economic development. Possible research topics may include: Small and medium enterprise growth Entrepreneurship ecosystems Business innovation Enterprise policy Start-up development Barriers faced by small businesses SME finance and support systems Focus on Inclusive and Prosperous Communities Leeds Business School particularly welcomes proposals that contribute to the university research theme of Inclusive and Prosperous Communities . One of the two research scholarships is reserved for a project aligned with this theme. This means applicants whose proposed research addresses inclusion, opportunity, community development, economic participation, or social prosperity may have a strong fit for one of the available awards. Research under this theme may focus on how communities can become more inclusive, resilient, economically active, and socially supported. It may also include research that examines barriers to prosperity and proposes practical ways to improve outcomes for individuals, businesses, and communities. Possible research directions could include: Inclusive economic development Community entrepreneurship Social mobility Local business growth Financial inclusion Regional inequality Inclusive workplaces Community-based innovation Responsible business practices that support local prosperity Benefits of the Leeds Business School PhD Studentships The information provided confirms that the opportunity is for two funded PhD studentships . While specific financial details such as stipend amount, tuition coverage, or additional funding support are not stated in the supplied information, the studentships are described as funded opportunities for full-time PhD study. Successful candidates will benefit from: A funded full-time PhD studentship A start date in October 2026 The opportunity to develop an original research project Supervision from academic experts in the proposed research area Membership in the Leeds Business School research community Access to a vibrant and diverse research environment The opportunity to engage with around 150 academics and researchers Interaction with an established doctoral student community Potential opportunity to undertake a small amount of undergraduate or Master’s teaching on an hourly basis A research environment connected to businesses, public bodies, universities, and community partners Who Should Apply? This opportunity is suitable for enthusiastic, motivated, and high-achieving individuals who are ready to undertake doctoral-level research. Applicants should be able to demonstrate: A strong interest in business, management, economics, media, sustainability, governance, enterprise, supply chains, or related fields The ability to develop a focused PhD research proposal Alignment with one or more of Leeds Business School’s research priority areas Academic potential for full-time doctoral study Interest in joining a research-led academic community Commitment to being based in Leeds full-time during the PhD programme The School is especially interested in applicants whose research can contribute to its strategic areas and help expand its research activity and profile. Location and Study Mode Successful candidates will be based at Leeds Business School , located at Leeds Beckett University’s City Campus in Leeds, United Kingdom . The PhD studentships are for full-time study , and successful applicants are expected to be based in Leeds full-time. This is important because the candidates will become part of the School’s research community and work closely with their supervisory teams. The stated start date for the PhD is October 2026 . However, the information provided notes that it may be possible for candidates to start slightly later if necessary. Supervision and Research Support Successful candidates will be allocated a supervisory team by Leeds Business School. The supervisors will be experts in the proposed research project area, helping candidates develop their research, refine their methodology, engage with existing literature, and progress through the PhD programme. The supervisory structure is an important part of the opportunity because applicants are expected to propose their own projects. Once selected, they will receive academic guidance from supervisors whose expertise aligns with the proposed research topic. Candidates will also join a wider doctoral community, giving them access to academic discussion, peer learning, research events, and support from other PhD students. Teaching Opportunities There may be an opportunity for successful PhD candidates to undertake a small amount of undergraduate or Master’s teaching on an hourly basis. However, the PhD programme of research and the candidate’s academic development will remain the main priority. Teaching may provide useful experience for candidates interested in academic careers, but it is not presented as the central purpose of the studentship. Deadline The deadline for this opportunity is not specified in the information provided . Applicants should check the official opportunity page or Leeds Beckett University’s application portal for the confirmed application deadline and full application instructions. Why This Opportunity Matters The Leeds Business School funded PhD studentships provide a valuable opportunity for individuals who want to conduct research that is academically strong and practically relevant. Because the School works with businesses, local authorities, NHS Trusts, universities, and other partners, successful candidates may have the chance to develop research that connects with real-world issues. The opportunity is especially strong for applicants whose interests sit at the intersection of business, society, sustainability, governance, enterprise, and inclusive economic development. It is also ideal for applicants who want to join a research community rather than study in isolation. With around 150 academics and researchers, as well as an established body of doctoral students, Leeds Business School offers a supportive environment for full-time doctoral study. How to Strengthen Your Application Applicants should prepare carefully because proposals that do not directly address the listed research areas will not be considered. To improve your chances, consider the following: Choose a research topic that clearly fits one of the listed areas Your proposal should directly align with Banking and Markets, Sustainability, Digital Media and Society, Responsible Governance and Organisational Behaviour, Supply Chain and Anchor Networks, or SME Development and Enterprise. Show how your research contributes to knowledge and practice Since Leeds Business School conducts applied business research, your proposal should explain both the academic value and practical relevance of your study. Consider the Inclusive and Prosperous Communities theme One scholarship is reserved for this area, so applicants with strong proposals linked to inclusion, community prosperity, local economic development, or social impact may be well-positioned. Make your research question specific and realistic A strong PhD proposal should be focused enough to complete within the programme while still offering original insight. Connect your proposal to the School’s expertise Your application should show that Leeds Business School is the right place for your research. Demonstrate readiness for full-time PhD study The selection team will likely be looking for candidates who are academically prepared, motivated, independent, and capable of sustained research. Final Thoughts The Leeds Business School Funded PhD Studentships 2026 offer an excellent opportunity for motivated researchers to pursue full-time doctoral study at Leeds Beckett University. With two funded studentships available, the opportunity is especially relevant for applicants whose research interests align with business, management, sustainability, governance, digital media, finance, supply chains, enterprise, and inclusive community development. Applicants should ensure that their proposals are closely connected to the School’s priority research areas, as proposals outside these areas will not be considered. For candidates with a strong research idea and a commitment to applied business research, this studentship offers a pathway into a vibrant academic environment and an established doctoral research community in the heart of Leeds. APPLY HERE. For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more Scholarship opportunities on OFY here. The post Leeds Business School Funded PhD Studentships 2026: Apply for Two Full-Time Research Scholarships at Leeds Beckett University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Cambridge INT Academic Career Development Fellow 2026 in Global Politics and International Relations (Middle East, North Africa, or South Asia)
summary: The University of Cambridge has announced applications for the prestigious INT Academic Career Development Fellowship 2026 in the field of Global Politics and International Relations, offering an exceptional academic opportunity for early-career scholars specializing in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) or South Asia. Hosted by the Department of Politics and International Studies (POLIS) in collaboration with Girton College, this highly competitive three-year fellowship provides scholars with the opportunity to advance cutting-edge research, contribute to world-class teaching, and engage in one of the most intellectually vibrant academic communities globally. Supported generously by the Isaac Newton Trust, the fellowship is designed to nurture outstanding emerging academics with strong research potential and a compelling future vision in politics and international studies. The position will begin on 1 September 2026 and is non-renewable after the completion of the three-year term. About the University of Cambridge POLIS Fellowship The Department of Politics and International Studies (POLIS) at the University of Cambridge is internationally recognized for excellence in political science, international relations, public policy, and area studies. The Academic Career Development Fellowship aims to support scholars whose work contributes significantly to understanding global politics and international relations, particularly in relation to: The Middle East and North Africa (MENA) South Asia The successful candidate will also become a Fellow of Girton College, one of Cambridge’s historic colleges known for its dynamic and inclusive academic environment. This dual appointment offers fellows the opportunity to engage deeply in both departmental and collegiate academic life. Fellowship Structure and Responsibilities The role is structured across two academic institutions within the University of Cambridge system: Allocation of Responsibilities Approximately 70% of the appointment is based within POLIS Approximately 30% is based at Girton College Within the POLIS portion: Around 40% of the time will be protected specifically for research activities The remaining time will focus on teaching, examinations, and academic service responsibilities At Girton College: Fellows will undertake agreed teaching responsibilities Supervision duties will normally not exceed 80 hours annually Research and Academic Expectations The University is seeking candidates with a strong and promising academic profile. Applicants must demonstrate: Expertise in global politics and international relations related to MENA or South Asia A strong publication and research record appropriate to career stage A compelling future research agenda The ability to contribute to the academic development of the subject area at Cambridge The fellowship is particularly suitable for emerging scholars aiming to establish themselves as leading academics in international politics and regional studies. Teaching Responsibilities The successful fellow will participate actively in teaching across undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Responsibilities may include: Delivering lectures Conducting seminars Supervising students Directing studies Participating in examinations The position requires strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary academic environment. Collegiate and Administrative Contributions In addition to research and teaching, fellows will: Participate in departmental governance and administration Contribute to the collegial life of POLIS Engage in academic activities at Girton College Collaborate with scholars across the wider University of Cambridge network The fellowship offers a rich environment for interdisciplinary collaboration and academic networking. Benefits of the Cambridge INT Academic Career Development Fellowship This fellowship provides substantial professional and academic advantages for early-career researchers. 1. Prestigious Cambridge Appointment Successful candidates become part of one of the world’s leading universities. 2. Dedicated Research Time The fellowship includes protected research time to support scholarly productivity and publication development. 3. Teaching and Mentorship Experience Participants gain valuable teaching experience at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. 4. Fellowship at Girton College The successful applicant will join a supportive collegiate academic community. 5. International Academic Exposure Cambridge offers access to leading researchers, global academic networks, conferences, and collaborative opportunities. 6. Career Advancement The fellowship is specifically designed to strengthen the academic career trajectory of emerging scholars. 7. Competitive Salary The salary range for the position is: £37,694 – £46,049 Eligibility Criteria Applicants should meet the following requirements: Hold a PhD in a relevant discipline Demonstrate expertise in global politics or international relations related to MENA or South Asia Show evidence of strong research achievement Possess excellent teaching and communication skills Demonstrate the ability to work independently and collaboratively Be capable of contributing to a multidisciplinary academic environment Diversity and Inclusion The University of Cambridge actively supports equality, diversity, and inclusion and encourages applications from all sections of society. Applicants from diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Work Eligibility Requirement The University states that all employees must be eligible to live and work in the United Kingdom. International applicants should therefore ensure they meet UK immigration and employment requirements. Application Process Interested candidates must apply online through the University of Cambridge recruitment portal. Applicants should: Register an account on the recruitment system Complete the online application Submit all required academic and professional materials Applicants should quote the following reference number in all correspondence: UE49748 Important Dates Application Deadline The closing date for applications is: Midnight on Thursday, 4 June 2026 Expected Interview Date Interviews are expected to take place on: Wednesday, 24 June 2026 Contact Information For questions regarding the role: Professor Duncan Kelly Email: djk36@cam.ac.uk For application process inquiries: bom@polis.cam.ac.uk Why This Fellowship Is Highly Competitive The University of Cambridge fellowship ecosystem is globally respected, and this opportunity provides an ideal platform for emerging scholars to develop independent academic careers. The combination of: Protected research time Teaching experience Collegiate fellowship International recognition Access to Cambridge’s scholarly network makes this one of the most attractive early-career academic opportunities in political science and international relations. Scholars focusing on geopolitical dynamics, governance, international diplomacy, conflict studies, regional politics, or comparative political systems in MENA and South Asia are especially well-positioned for this role. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post University of Cambridge INT Academic Career Development Fellow 2026 in Global Politics and International Relations (Middle East, North Africa, or South Asia) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNCCD COP17 Women Delegates Fund 2026: Negotiation Training and Participation Support for Women Leaders from Developing Countries
summary: The United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) has officially launched the second round of the Women Delegates Fund (WDF) Training Programme ahead of COP17, creating a major opportunity for women leaders from developing countries to strengthen their participation in global environmental negotiations. As preparations intensify for UNCCD COP17 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, the initiative seeks to empower women delegates with the knowledge, negotiation skills, leadership capacity, and institutional support needed to actively engage in international climate and land governance discussions. The programme builds on the success of the first Women Delegates Fund training organized during CRIC23 in Panama City and continues the UNCCD’s commitment to gender-responsive participation and inclusive decision-making in environmental diplomacy. For women working in environmental policy, climate action, sustainable development, land restoration, drought resilience, and international negotiations, this initiative represents a powerful opportunity to gain global exposure and contribute meaningfully to one of the world’s most important environmental conventions. About the UNCCD COP17 Conference UNCCD COP17 will take place in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, from 17–28 August 2026 under the official theme: “Restoring Land, Restoring Hope.” The Conference of the Parties (COP) to the UNCCD is the global decision-making platform for the only legally binding international treaty focused specifically on combating land degradation and drought. COP17 is expected to gather: Government representatives Civil society organizations Indigenous Peoples Women’s organizations Youth leaders Scientists and researchers International development actors Environmental advocates Private sector stakeholders The conference will focus on advancing solutions for: Land restoration Sustainable land management Drought resilience Climate adaptation Gender-responsive environmental governance Community-led restoration initiatives As global environmental challenges continue to intensify, COP17 will provide a critical platform for shaping future international policies and commitments. What is the UNCCD Women Delegates Fund (WDF)? The Women Delegates Fund is an initiative designed to increase women’s participation and leadership within the UNCCD negotiation and implementation process. The programme contributes directly to the implementation of the UNCCD Gender Action Plan (GAP) and its Roadmap, both of which aim to strengthen women’s voices and representation across all levels of environmental decision-making. The WDF recognizes that women are often at the forefront of environmental stewardship, land restoration, food security, and community resilience efforts, yet remain underrepresented in international policy negotiations. Through targeted support, mentorship, and negotiation training, the initiative aims to bridge this gap and ensure more inclusive participation at COP17 and beyond. Objectives of the Programme The Women Delegates Fund Training Programme has several strategic goals, including: 1. Strengthening Women’s Leadership The programme seeks to empower women delegates to take active leadership roles in environmental diplomacy and multilateral negotiations. 2. Enhancing Negotiation Skills Participants will receive specialized negotiation support and capacity-building activities to help them effectively engage during COP17 discussions. 3. Promoting Meaningful Participation The initiative supports women’s full and effective participation in UNCCD processes and related environmental governance mechanisms. 4. Expanding Professional Networks Participants will connect with fellow women delegates, policymakers, negotiators, and environmental leaders from different regions of the world. 5. Supporting Gender Equality in Environmental Governance The programme advances gender-responsive leadership and representation within international environmental institutions. Benefits of the UNCCD Women Delegates Fund Programme Selected women delegates will receive a range of benefits designed to support their participation before and during COP17. Participation Support for COP17 Successful applicants will receive support that facilitates their participation in the conference process. Capacity-Building and Negotiation Training Participants will benefit from tailored activities aimed at strengthening: Negotiation techniques Policy engagement skills Understanding of UNCCD processes Multilateral diplomacy knowledge Leadership capabilities Pre-COP Briefings Delegates will gain access to preparatory sessions and briefings ahead of COP17 to improve readiness and strategic participation. Networking Opportunities Participants will engage with women delegates and environmental leaders from around the world, helping build lasting professional relationships and collaborative opportunities. International Exposure The programme provides valuable international experience and visibility in global environmental governance. Who Can Apply? The initiative is specifically open to women delegates from developing country Parties that are eligible under the UNCCD Special Trust Fund for Participation. Priority consideration will be given to delegates from Least Developed Countries (LDCs). Governments from all Regional Implementation Annexes are encouraged to nominate one eligible woman delegate as part of their official COP17 delegation. Eligibility Criteria Applicants should meet the following conditions: Be a woman delegate Be nominated by their government Belong to a developing country Party eligible under the UNCCD Special Trust Fund for Participation Be part of the official COP17 delegation Demonstrate interest or involvement in environmental governance, sustainable land management, climate policy, or related fields Priority will be given to applicants from Least Developed Countries (LDCs). Required Application Documents Applications must be submitted through National Focal Points and should include: Official nomination letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) or short biography of the proposed delegate Applicants are encouraged to ensure all documentation is complete and officially endorsed before submission. Why This Opportunity Matters Women continue to play critical roles in sustainable land management, agriculture, biodiversity conservation, drought response, and climate resilience. However, they often face barriers to representation in global policymaking spaces. The UNCCD Women Delegates Fund directly addresses this imbalance by creating pathways for women to engage more effectively in international negotiations and decision-making. Participation in this programme can significantly strengthen delegates’ expertise, confidence, networks, and long-term influence in environmental governance. It also contributes to broader international efforts to achieve gender equality and inclusive climate leadership. COP17 Theme: “Restoring Land, Restoring Hope” The 2026 conference theme highlights the urgent need to restore degraded ecosystems and strengthen resilience against drought and environmental degradation. Land restoration remains central to achieving: Food security Climate resilience Biodiversity conservation Sustainable livelihoods Community stability The inclusion of women leaders and negotiators in these discussions is essential for developing inclusive and sustainable solutions. Deadline for Applications Applicants are encouraged to submit their nominations as early as possible through their National Focal Points. The official announcement does not specify a final deadline publicly in the provided information. Interested delegates should consult their National Focal Points or the official UNCCD website for submission timelines and procedural guidance. How to Learn More Interested applicants can access additional details through the official UNCCD channels and communication platforms. For media or general inquiries, contact: UNCCD Press Office Email: press@unccd.int Telephone: +49-228 / 815-2820 Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Training Programme opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post UNCCD COP17 Women Delegates Fund 2026: Negotiation Training and Participation Support for Women Leaders from Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Henry Moore Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Sculpture Research, Exhibitions, Conservation, and Publications Worldwide
summary: The Henry Moore Foundation has officially opened applications for its prestigious Henry Moore Grants and Fellowships programme, offering funding support to non-profit organizations, sculpture researchers, academics, and conservators across the globe. The programme is designed to strengthen the growth, preservation, research, and public appreciation of sculpture in historical, modern, and contemporary contexts. This internationally recognized funding initiative supports projects that expand conversations around sculpture while improving access to sculptural knowledge, collections, exhibitions, and conservation practices. Organizations and individuals working within sculpture-related disciplines now have the opportunity to secure financial support for impactful projects ranging from exhibitions and conferences to conservation work and long-term research initiatives. The Henry Moore Foundation continues its longstanding commitment to advancing sculpture through grants that encourage innovation, scholarship, and public engagement. Whether applicants are developing exhibitions, publishing critical research, conserving sculptural collections, or organizing academic conferences, the foundation provides several categories of funding tailored to diverse project needs. Applications are accepted four times each year, allowing institutions and researchers flexibility in planning their projects and timelines. What the Henry Moore Grants Programme Supports The Henry Moore Grants programme supports projects that contribute meaningfully to sculpture studies, public engagement, conservation, interpretation, and accessibility. Funding is available for both organizations and eligible individuals under specific categories. The programme welcomes applications for: Sculpture exhibitions Exhibition catalogues Sculpture commissions Conferences and lectures Research initiatives Non-commercial publications Collection acquisitions Sculpture conservation projects Cataloguing and display development Travel and archival research The foundation is particularly interested in projects that generate new perspectives on sculpture history or contribute to broader public understanding and appreciation of sculpture as an artistic and cultural practice. Categories of Henry Moore Grants New Projects and Commissions This category supports exhibitions, exhibition catalogues, and sculpture commissions that encourage new thinking about sculpture or increase public engagement with sculpture history. Projects may include museum exhibitions, contemporary sculpture installations, public commissions, or scholarly exhibition catalogues. Maximum funding available: £20,000 Organizations applying under this category should demonstrate how their projects contribute to sculpture discourse, public learning, or artistic innovation. Acquisitions and Collections Museums, galleries, and collecting institutions can apply for support to acquire sculptural works, conserve existing collections, or improve collection accessibility through cataloguing and display initiatives. Funding may support: Sculpture acquisitions Conservation treatment Documentation and cataloguing Collection display improvements Maximum funding available: £20,000 This category helps institutions preserve important sculptural heritage while improving public access to collections. Conferences, Lectures, and Publications Organizations planning academic or public-facing events related to sculpture may apply under this category. Funding is also available for non-commercial books and journals focusing on sculpture studies. Eligible projects include: Academic conferences Public lecture series Research publications Sculpture journals Symposiums Please note that exhibition catalogues must be submitted under the New Projects and Commissions category instead. Maximum funding available: £5,000 Long-Term Research Grants The Henry Moore Foundation also supports extensive research projects that require funding across multiple years. This category is ideal for major scholarly initiatives such as permanent collection catalogues or long-term archival research. Maximum funding available: £20,000 Applicants should provide detailed timelines, methodologies, and long-term impact plans for their research activities. Research and Travel Grants for Individuals Unlike other categories restricted to organizations, the Research and Travel Grants category is specifically designed for individual scholars and conservators. Eligible applicants include: Sculpture historians Academics Conservators Funding supports: Research costs Travel expenses Photography Archival access Sculpture conservation research Historical and interpretive studies Maximum funding available: £2,500 The foundation clearly states that it does not support projects intended to develop or inform an artist’s own practice or creative work. Projects That Are Not Eligible The Henry Moore Foundation outlines several project types that do not qualify for funding. These include: Residency programmes Building or central operational costs Traditional memorials and figurative statues Commercial publishing projects Community gardens Wellbeing activities Play equipment Independent artistic practice development Community project proposals are only considered when submitted by non-profit arts organizations. Application Process Applications for Henry Moore Grants are managed through the Flexi-Grant portal. Applicants must register for an account before submitting their proposals. Organizations should ensure registration requests are completed at least one week before application deadlines. Late registration requests will automatically be moved to the next funding round. Applicants can submit, manage, and review applications directly through the online grants portal. Why You Should Apply The Henry Moore Foundation remains one of the world’s leading supporters of sculpture research, preservation, and public engagement. Receiving funding from the foundation can significantly strengthen institutional projects, increase visibility, and enhance long-term impact within the arts and cultural sector. For researchers and organizations dedicated to sculpture history, conservation, exhibitions, and public education, this opportunity offers not only financial support but also international recognition and credibility. Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong proposals that clearly explain the significance, outcomes, and public value of their projects. Deadline: 1 June, 23:00 GMT APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here The post Henry Moore Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Sculpture Research, Exhibitions, Conservation, and Publications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IGAD Regional Training of Trainers 2026 on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade in Kenya | Fully Supported Opportunity for MSMEs, Entrepreneurs, Women & Youth Leaders Across East Africa
summary: The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), through the Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I, has officially opened applications for the Regional Training of Trainers (ToTs) on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade using the Gender Action Learning System (GALS) methodology. Scheduled to take place from July 6–8, 2026 in Nairobi, Kenya, this highly intensive regional programme aims to equip entrepreneurs, MSME leaders, gender champions, and digital trade advocates from IGAD Member States with practical skills to strengthen gender-responsive digital trade ecosystems across East Africa. The initiative comes at a critical time when digital transformation, cross-border trade, women’s economic inclusion, and youth entrepreneurship are becoming central to Africa’s economic future under frameworks such as the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) and IGAD Vision 2050. This opportunity is particularly valuable for entrepreneurs and MSME leaders interested in: Digital trade E-commerce Women’s economic empowerment Financial inclusion Climate-resilient entrepreneurship Cross-border trade Youth-led enterprise development Participants selected for this programme will become recognized Gender Champions capable of mentoring and training women and youth-led enterprises across their countries. About the IGAD HDMI Project The Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I is a multinational intervention implemented under IGAD’s Economic Cooperation and Regional Integration Division (ECRID). The project operates in: Djibouti Ethiopia Somalia South Sudan Its broader mission is to strengthen economic integration across the IGAD region by: Unlocking digital market opportunities Supporting e-commerce ecosystems Expanding digital financial systems Promoting digital entrepreneurship Enhancing cybersecurity and digital trust systems The project aligns with: AfCFTA implementation IGAD Vision 2050 IGAD Strategy 2026–2030 By improving access to digital markets, the initiative aims to: Increase youth employment Promote women-led enterprise growth Facilitate cross-border commerce Encourage innovation-driven entrepreneurship Why This Training Matters Despite rapid digital transformation across Africa, many women and youth entrepreneurs still face serious barriers, including: Limited access to digital technologies Lack of digital financial services Low participation in e-commerce Restricted access to regional and international markets Socio-cultural limitations Digital literacy gaps This regional training seeks to address these challenges by creating a network of Gender Champions who can help women and youth MSMEs access digital trade opportunities and participate effectively in the digital economy. The programme focuses on empowering participants with tools to: Promote gender-sensitive digital trade Mentor local entrepreneurs Strengthen inclusive market participation Expand digital entrepreneurship ecosystems What is the Gender Action Learning System (GALS)? The Gender Action Learning System (GALS) is a community-led empowerment methodology designed to: Address gender inequalities Support inclusive economic participation Encourage social transformation Improve entrepreneurship outcomes In this programme, GALS will be used to: Address socio-cultural barriers Promote gender equity in digital trade Enhance women’s participation in regional markets Build inclusive business ecosystems Participants will learn practical methods for using GALS within entrepreneurship, mentoring, and digital trade training environments. Objectives of the Regional Training The training aims to build a stronger regional ecosystem for inclusive digital trade and entrepreneurship. Specific objectives include: 1. Building Capacity of Gender Champions The programme seeks to train 21 Gender Champions from across IGAD Member States on: Gender-responsive digital trade E-commerce systems Digital entrepreneurship Inclusive market participation 2. Strengthening Practical Mentorship Skills Participants will gain: Facilitation skills Mentoring techniques GALS implementation skills Community engagement strategies 3. Promoting Women and Youth MSMEs The initiative aims to increase: Access to digital markets Digital financial inclusion E-commerce participation Cross-border trade engagement 4. Expanding Knowledge on Digital Financial Systems Participants will develop understanding of: Digital payment systems E-commerce tools Online business ecosystems Digital trade regulations Training Structure and Format The programme will be delivered in two phases. Phase 1: Intensive In-Person Regional Workshop Location: Nairobi, Kenya Dates: July 6–8, 2026 Duration: 3 full residential training days This phase includes: Practical exercises Interactive learning Hands-on facilitation training Group collaboration Mentorship preparation Phase 2: Online Follow-Up Sessions After the physical workshop, participants will engage in: Online learning sessions Knowledge-sharing discussions Continued mentorship support Digital networking activities These sessions will be facilitated through IGAD social platforms. Important Training Rules IGAD has emphasized that this is a highly intensive Training of Trainers programme. Participants are expected to: Attend all sessions fully Participate actively Commit completely throughout the programme Engage in all practical activities Importantly: Use of mobile phones during sessions will not be permitted This policy is intended to encourage full concentration and participation. Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will gain several important benefits. 1. Recognition as Regional Gender Champions Participants will officially become Gender Champions within the IGAD regional ecosystem. 2. Regional Networking Opportunities Participants will connect with: Entrepreneurs Policy experts Gender advocates Digital trade specialists MSME leaders across East Africa 3. Advanced Skills in Digital Trade The programme provides knowledge in: E-commerce Digital market access Financial inclusion Cross-border digital trade 4. Mentorship and Facilitation Skills Participants will gain skills needed to: Train others Facilitate workshops Mentor entrepreneurs Support MSMEs 5. Exposure to Regional Economic Integration Participants will deepen understanding of: AfCFTA opportunities Regional trade systems Digital economic ecosystems 6. IGAD-Covered Training Costs IGAD will cover training-related costs for selected participants according to organizational policies. Expected Responsibilities After Training Selected participants are expected to contribute actively after the programme. Responsibilities include: Conducting in-country trainings Mentoring women and youth MSMEs Supporting financial inclusion initiatives Promoting digital entrepreneurship Expanding inclusive digital trade participation This ensures that the programme creates long-term regional impact. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Nationality Applicants must: Be nationals of an IGAD Member State Business Requirement Applicants should: Be actively running an MSME Have potential for digital or cross-border trade Documentation Applicants must possess: A valid business license Educational Qualification Applicants should have: At least a certificate of secondary education Experience and Interest Applicants should demonstrate: Interest in digital trade Experience in entrepreneurship or e-commerce Commitment to gender equality Mentorship Commitment Participants must: Be willing to train and mentor others after the programme Eligible IGAD Member States The opportunity is open to nationals from IGAD Member States, including: Djibouti Ethiopia Kenya Somalia South Sudan Sudan Uganda Eritrea Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit: Completed online application form Copy of business license Motivation statement (maximum 300 words) Short business description (maximum 100 words) The motivation statement should explain: Why the applicant is suitable Interest in digital trade Commitment to women and youth empowerment Application Deadline The application deadline is: June 5, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early because only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. How to Apply Applicants should complete the online application through the official IGAD application portal. Application Link: https://igad.int/hdmicallforapplication/ Why This Opportunity Stands Out This programme stands out because it combines: Digital trade Gender inclusion Regional integration Entrepreneurship Mentorship Cross-border economic development Unlike many entrepreneurship trainings, this initiative focuses on building long-term regional trainers and ecosystem leaders capable of multiplying impact across East Africa. Participants will not only gain personal skills but will also become catalysts for inclusive economic transformation within their communities and countries. Click HERE to learn more about the IGAD Regional Training of Trainers 2026 in Kenya. Conclusion The IGAD Regional Training of Trainers on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade is a highly strategic regional opportunity for entrepreneurs, MSMEs, women leaders, youth innovators, and digital trade advocates across East Africa. By empowering Gender Champions with skills in digital trade, mentorship, financial inclusion, and entrepreneurship support, the programme contributes directly to inclusive economic growth and regional integration. For entrepreneurs and changemakers interested in driving gender-responsive digital transformation across Africa, this programme represents an outstanding platform for leadership, impact, and regional collaboration. Discover more global African and Women opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY). The post IGAD Regional Training of Trainers 2026 on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade in Kenya | Fully Supported Opportunity for MSMEs, Entrepreneurs, Women Youth Leaders Across East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CIVICUS Digital and Civic Space Strategy Consultancy 2026: Global Remote Opportunity for Digital Rights and Civil Society Experts
summary: The global civil society alliance CIVICUS has officially opened applications for an international consultancy opportunity focused on designing and Unit operationalizing its Digital Civic Space Strategy. This high-level consultancy seeks experienced researchers, strategists, policy experts, and civil society professionals who can help shape the future of civic and democratic space in the digital age. The consultancy is being coordinated through the Digital Democracy Initiative (DDI), a global programme that emerged from Denmark’s Tech for Democracy initiative. The opportunity is fully remote and open to qualified applicants worldwide. Selected consultants will work over a seven-month period to develop thematic advisory notes, facilitate global consultations, and support the drafting of CIVICUS’ long-term strategy on digital civic space. About CIVICUS and Its Global Mission CIVICUS is one of the world’s leading alliances dedicated to strengthening citizen action and defending civic freedoms. The organisation has more than 15,000 members spread across 175 countries and works closely with activists, civil society organisations, grassroots movements, and democratic institutions. CIVICUS focuses on expanding civic and democratic space globally, especially in contexts where freedoms of expression, association, and assembly are under threat. The organisation places particular emphasis on amplifying voices from the Global South and supporting collective approaches to sustainable democratic change. The alliance works around five strategic objectives: Generating timely knowledge and research on civic space Coordinating advocacy efforts to protect democratic freedoms Supporting activists and organisations facing threats Strengthening public discourse around civic participation Building collective power among affected communities and movements Understanding the Digital Democracy Initiative (DDI) The Digital Democracy Initiative was created in response to growing threats to democracy and civic freedoms in digital environments. Governments, corporations, and hostile actors increasingly use surveillance technologies, internet shutdowns, AI-driven disinformation, and digital manipulation to silence civil society voices. At the same time, digital technology also creates opportunities for: Cross-border movement building Real-time accountability and transparency Amplification of marginalised voices Civic participation and innovation Digital organising and advocacy The DDI consortium includes globally recognised organisations such as: Access Now CIVICUS Digital Defenders Partnership European Partnership for Democracy Witness IWGIA Global Focus Fundo de Mujeres del Sur After two years of implementation across multiple regions including Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, East Asia, Latin America, Eastern Europe, Central Asia, and the Middle East, the initiative has generated significant evidence and learning that now forms the foundation for the CIVICUS Digital Civic Space Strategy. What the Consultant Will Do The selected consultant or consulting team will play a central role in transforming CIVICUS’ strategic framework into a practical, implementation-ready strategy. The consultancy has two major streams of work: 1. Development of Five Thematic Advisory Notes The consultant will produce five detailed advisory notes, each between 8 and 12 pages long. These documents will provide strategic and analytical guidance on critical digital civic space themes. The five thematic areas include: Digital Security and Resilience This area focuses on: Digital safety for activists and organisations Emergency digital response systems Psychosocial resilience Legal compliance in digital spaces Civil society peer training networks Information Integrity, Disinformation, and Electoral Civic Space Topics include: Fact-checking systems Deepfake detection AI-generated misinformation Electoral integrity technologies Platform accountability advocacy Civic Technology and Digital Democracy This note will explore: AI tools for civic engagement Civic participation technologies Accountability platforms Low-bandwidth participation systems Civic tech innovation ecosystems Digital Rights and Policy Advocacy Key areas include: Tracking digital repression globally Digital legislation advocacy AI governance Human rights policy engagement Coalition building Digital Inclusion and Equity This pillar focuses on: Supporting digitally marginalised communities Feminist digital organising Regional equity Hybrid civic participation Digital literacy as a civic competency Multi-Stakeholder Consultation Process A major aspect of the consultancy involves facilitating consultations with diverse stakeholder groups. The consultant will engage: CIVICUS staff DDI programme participants Digital rights organisations CIVICUS members and networks Civil society actors across six global regions The consultation process may involve: Virtual focus groups Interviews Surveys Regional roundtables Stakeholder mapping exercises Particular emphasis will be placed on ensuring participation from Global South communities and multilingual engagement where necessary. Deliverables Expected from the Consultant At the end of the consultancy, the selected applicant will submit: Five thematic advisory notes Consultation frameworks and instruments Stakeholder engagement reports A consultation synthesis report A consolidated strategy input package Draft versions of the Digital Civic Space Strategy Presentation of findings to CIVICUS leadership and staff Required Qualifications and Experience CIVICUS is looking for highly experienced professionals with expertise across digital rights, policy, strategy, and civil society development. Applicants should demonstrate: Strong analytical and writing skills Experience with strategic planning and policy analysis Expertise in digital civic space and technology governance Experience facilitating participatory consultations Familiarity with global digital rights advocacy Understanding of challenges facing activists and civil society organisations Ability to communicate complex concepts to non-technical audiences Additional language skills in French, Arabic, Portuguese, or Spanish are considered highly valuable. Duration, Location, and Deadline Location: Global / Remote Consultancy Duration: 7 Months Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit: A cover letter Detailed CV Technical proposal and methodology Work plan and timeline Budget proposal Two relevant work samples Three professional references Applications should be sent via email andmust use the subject line: “Proposal: Digital Civic Space Strategy” Incomplete applications or submissions sent through external download links may not be considered. Why This Opportunity Matters This consultancy represents a major opportunity for digital rights experts, civic technology professionals, and international development strategists to contribute directly to shaping the future of global civic space. As digital threats to democracy continue to evolve, organisations like CIVICUS are investing heavily in long-term, evidence-based, and globally inclusive strategies. Consultants selected for this project will help influence international civil society responses to issues such as AI governance, digital surveillance, disinformation, civic technology, and democratic resilience. For professionals passionate about democracy, technology policy, and human rights, this consultancy provides a chance to work with one of the world’s most influential civil society alliances while contributing to transformative global impact. Deadline: 31st May,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here The post CIVICUS Digital and Civic Space Strategy Consultancy 2026: Global Remote Opportunity for Digital Rights and Civil Society Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mandela Rhodes Foundation Äänit Prize 2026: Up to $100,000 Funding for African Social Impact Innovators
summary: The Mandela Rhodes Foundation has officially opened applications for the 2026 Äänit Prize, a prestigious African social impact funding opportunity designed to support innovative changemakers creating sustainable solutions for marginalized communities across the continent. The prize, which offers up to $100,000 in annual funding, is open exclusively to Mandela Rhodes Scholarship alumni and Rhodes Scholarship alumni. Through this initiative, the Foundation aims to empower visionary African leaders whose businesses, nonprofit organizations, or hybrid initiatives are driving meaningful transformation in society. The Äänit Prize reflects the legacy and values of Nelson Mandela by investing in young African innovators who are solving pressing social, economic, environmental, and developmental challenges through impactful and sustainable initiatives. What is the Mandela Rhodes Foundation Äänit Prize? The Äänit Prize is a flagship social impact award established by the Mandela Rhodes Foundation to identify, support, and scale initiatives that create measurable impact for vulnerable and underserved populations in Africa. The competition recognizes projects and ventures that are: Socially impactful Sustainable and feasible Innovative and scalable Led by capable African changemakers Focused on solving pressing African challenges For the 2026 edition, the Foundation is broadening the scope of the award to also recognize alumni who are driving transformative work within existing organizations, whether in the nonprofit, private, public, or hybrid sectors. This expansion acknowledges that impactful change can happen not only through entrepreneurship but also through leadership and innovation inside established institutions. According to Judy Sikuza, CEO of the Mandela Rhodes Foundation: “Nelson Mandela really believed in the potential of young people. He believed that if we invest in young people and their dreams and ideas, we will see an acceleration of the transformation of the continent.” Funding Value and Benefits of the Äänit Prize 2026 Selected winners will share funding of up to $100,000 annually. The prize supports between one and four winners each year depending on the quality and scale of applications received. Benefits include: Significant financial support for scaling impact initiatives Recognition from one of Africa’s leading leadership institutions Increased visibility for social enterprises and nonprofit projects Access to a network of influential African leaders and innovators Enhanced credibility for fundraising and partnerships Opportunity to contribute to transformative development across Africa The prize welcomes initiatives operating at different stages of development, from early-stage ideas to established ventures with proven impact. Focus Areas of the Äänit Prize The Mandela Rhodes Foundation is interested in initiatives aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals and broader African development priorities. The 10 priority impact areas include: 1. Access to Affordable Housing Projects improving housing access, affordability, and living conditions for underserved communities. 2. Agriculture and Food Security Innovations strengthening agricultural productivity, food access, and rural livelihoods. 3. Climate Change Solutions addressing climate resilience, adaptation, mitigation, and environmental sustainability. 4. Conflict and Terrorism Initiatives promoting peacebuilding, social cohesion, and conflict prevention. 5. Education Programs improving educational access, quality, skills development, and youth empowerment. 6. Energy Access Renewable energy and clean energy projects targeting underserved populations. 7. Financial Services Financial inclusion initiatives expanding access to banking, insurance, and economic opportunities. 8. Healthcare, Water and Sanitation Innovations improving health outcomes, clean water access, and sanitation systems. 9. Infrastructure Projects strengthening community infrastructure and essential public services. 10. Technology and Data Tech-driven initiatives leveraging innovation and data for social impact. Who Can Apply for the Äänit Prize 2026? To qualify, applicants must meet the following criteria: Alumni Requirement Applicants must be: Mandela Rhodes Alumni from 2005 onwards, or Rhodes Scholar Alumni from 2005 onwards Leadership Requirement Applicants must be: Founders Co-founders, or Members of the executive team of the organization or venture they are submitting. Impact Requirement Applicants must demonstrate clear or potential contribution to at least one of the designated impact areas in Africa. Commitment Requirement Successful applicants are expected to remain involved in their ventures for at least two years following the award. Types of Eligible Initiatives The Äänit Prize supports: Nonprofit organizations Social enterprises For-profit businesses with social impact Hybrid organizations Existing organizations driving innovative change Early-stage and established ventures The Foundation strongly encourages creative, scalable, and sustainable solutions that address systemic African challenges. Step-by-Step Application Process for the Äänit Prize 2026 Step 1: Indicate Interest Application Period: 20 May 2026 – 17 June 2026 Applicants must submit a short motivation explaining: The social impact of their initiative Their vision and implementation strategy Why they are suited to lead the initiative The Foundation emphasizes accessibility and encourages applicants not to self-exclude. Step 2: Invitation to Apply The Foundation reviews all expressions of interest and invites selected candidates to submit full applications. Step 3: Submit Full Application Application Period: 1 July 2026 – 21 July 2026 Applicants will submit: Written project statement Project or business plan (maximum 12 pages) Supporting documentation Applications are reviewed by experienced evaluators and experts. Step 4: Interview and Pitch Longlisted candidates will participate in interviews during the week of 17 August 2026. Applicants must also deliver a short pitch highlighting the value proposition of their initiative. Step 5: Proposal Refinement Final-round candidates will receive reviewer feedback and have 48 hours to improve their proposals and submit a two-minute pitch video. Step 6: Final Selection An independent external judging panel determines: Final winners Number of awardees Distribution of funding Winners will be announced publicly on 18 September 2026. APPLY HERE Important Dates for the Äänit Prize 2026 Expression of Interest Opens: 20 May 2026 Expression of Interest Deadline: 17 June 2026 Full Application Period: 1 July – 21 July 2026 Interviews: Week of 17 August 2026 Final Announcement: 18 September 2026 Why the Ääänit Prize Matters for Africa The Ääänit Prize is more than a funding opportunity. It is a platform for empowering African leaders who are developing solutions tailored to African realities. The initiative recognizes that entrepreneurship and innovation can drive inclusive development, reduce inequality, create jobs, improve livelihoods, and strengthen resilience across the continent. By supporting mission-driven leaders, the Mandela Rhodes Foundation continues to invest in the next generation of African transformation. Applicants with bold ideas, strong leadership capacity, and a commitment to social impact are encouraged to apply. For more information about this grant visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here and applications, visit the Mandela Rhodes Foundation official website.
